mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 2.0% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls below 2.0% and remains there for at least 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 1.82% suggests easing environment
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress corporate earnings expectations and increase equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as investors reduce equity allocations.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.4 percentage points within a 3-month window
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 4.0% is near historical lows
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or correlation between UNRATE changes and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating retail sales growth combined with rising housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and capital formation, signaling sustained economic expansion that supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 501971M and HOUST at 1350K suggest solid demand
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with stable monetary policy
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX fails to outperform baseline in periods when both RSAFS and HOUST are above their 6-month moving averages.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production signals weakening supply-side capacity utilization and reduced corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors anticipate earnings downgrades and margin compression.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines by more than 0.5% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 107.3006 requires trend analysis
regime_hint: early-stage economic slowdown or supply shock
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating payroll employment growth combined with stable inflation signals a tight labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations rise.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS grows by more than 200K month-over-month while CPIAUCSL growth remains below 0.3% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 148912K and CPIAUCSL at 250.857 suggest labor strength without runaway inflation
failure_condition: The interaction signal produces Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX underperforms when both conditions are met in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Declining real GDP growth signals weakening aggregate demand and reduced corporate earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as investors reduce equity risk exposure and rotate to defensive assets.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth rate falls below 1.5% annualized over the most recent quarter
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 17371.854B requires quarterly growth rate calculation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 58% of instances when GDPC1 growth is below 1.5%, or correlation is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation measured by CPIAUCSL combined with elevated federal funds rates signals monetary tightening that increases real discount rates and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors anticipate further rate hikes.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises by more than 0.4% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.5%
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 1.82% is below threshold but inflation dynamics matter
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: The interaction signal produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive returns in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained industrial production growth combined with rising employment signals robust supply-side capacity expansion and productivity gains, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as investors anticipate margin expansion and earnings growth.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO grows by more than 0.3% month-over-month for 2 consecutive months while PAYEMS growth exceeds 150K month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Both series show current strength suggesting supply-side momentum
regime_hint: mid-to-late cycle expansion with productivity gains
failure_condition: The interaction signal produces Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX underperforms baseline in more than 50% of instances when both conditions are met.
